twitter_posts.title:
New on the Engineering Blog: How we designed Claude Code auto mode.

Many Claude Code users let Claude work without permission prompts. Auto mode is a safer middle ground: we built and tested classifi
twitter_posts.description:
New on the Engineering Blog: How we designed Claude Code auto mode.

Many Claude Code users let Claude work without permission prompts. Auto mode is a safer middle ground: we built and tested classifiers that make approval decisions instead.

Read more: anthropic.com/engineering/
twitter_posts.author: Anthropic

twitter_posts.title: R to @AnthropicAI: We find that since November 2025, consumer use has become less concentrated: the top 10 tasks now make up 19% of conversations, down from 24%. We also see a rise in personal queries
twitter_posts.description:
We find that since November 2025, consumer use has become less concentrated: the top 10 tasks now make up 19% of conversations, down from 24%. We also see a rise in personal queries, and continued convergence in adoption rates in the US.

Read more: anthropic.com/research/econo…
twitter_posts.author: Anthropic

twitter_posts.title:
New from the Anthropic Economic Index: how people’s use of Claude changes with experience.

Longer-term users are more likely to iterate carefully with Claude, and less likely to hand it full autonomy
twitter_posts.description:
New from the Anthropic Economic Index: how people’s use of Claude changes with experience.

Longer-term users are more likely to iterate carefully with Claude, and less likely to hand it full autonomy. They attempt higher-value tasks, and receive more successful responses.
twitter_posts.author: Anthropic
